code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "rises to",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Build a map of predicate -> object for easy lookup
        pred_obj = {tr.predicate: tr.object for tr in group}
        handled = set()
        parts = []

        # Helper to clean quoted objects and strip units in parentheses
        def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
            o = o.strip('"').strip("'")
            # remove surrounding parentheses like (square metres)
            if '(' in o and ')' in o:
                o = o.split('(')[0].strip()
            return o

        # ----- Special combined handling for cost, floorCount, floorArea -----
        if all(p in pred_obj for p in ("cost", "floorCount", "floorArea")):
            cost = clean_obj(pred_obj["cost"])
            floor_cnt = clean_obj(pred_obj["floorCount"])
            floor_area = clean_obj(pred_obj["floorArea"])
            sentence = (f"{cost} is the cost to build {subject}, with its {floor_cnt} "
                        f"floors and {floor_area} square metres.")
            sentences.append(sentence)
            continue

        # ----- Leader predicate with more natural phrasing -----
        if "leader" in pred_obj:
            obj_clean = clean_obj(pred_obj["leader"])
            # Prefer "the leader in X is Y" style
            parts.append(f"The leader in {subject} is {obj_clean}")
            handled.add("leader")

        # ----- Capital and language handling (inverted) -----
        if "capital" in pred_obj:
            parts.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {clean_obj(pred_obj['capital'])}")
            handled.add("capital")
        if "language" in pred_obj:
            parts.append(f"{clean_obj(pred_obj['language'])} is the language of {subject}")
            handled.add("language")

        # ----- General predicate handling -----
        order = ["location", "height", "architect", "buildingType", "completionDate",
                 "buildingStartDate", "inaugurationDate", "owner", "formerName",
                 "floorCount", "floorArea", "cost", "tenant", "isPartOf", "country",
                 "currentTenants", "address", "birthPlace", "deathPlace", "bedCount",
                 "region", "state", "website", "yearOfConstruction",
                 "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber",
                 "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces", "significantBuilding",
                 "governingBody", "chancellor", "governmentType", "currency",
                 "ethnicGroup", "origin", "significantProject", "foundationPlace",
                 "demonym", "numberOfRooms", "keyPerson", "architecture", "postalCode"]
        for pred in order:
            if pred in handled or pred not in pred_obj:
                continue
            verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
            obj = clean_obj(pred_obj[pred])
            if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj} floors"
            elif pred == "cost":
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            else:
                clause = f"{verb} {obj}"
            parts.append(clause)
            handled.add(pred)

        # Build the final sentence for this subject
        if parts:
            # If the first clause already contains the subject, avoid repetition
            first = parts[0].lower()
            if subject.lower() in first or first.startswith("the "):
                sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

                  else:
